evals.name: Basic team onboarding (Kustomize)
evals.prompt: Onboard a new team called 'checkout' onto our Argo CD cluster. They have a Git repo at https://github.com/acme/checkout-svc.git with Kustomize overlays in overlays/dev and overlays/production. They need two namespaces: checkout-dev and checkout-prod. Their SSO group is 'checkout-developers'. They should be able to sync their own apps but not access other teams' namespaces. Generate the AppProject, Application for dev, and RBAC configuration.
expectations: Output includes kind: AppProject with sourceRepos restricted to https://github.com/acme/checkout-svc.git
AppProject destinations are scoped to checkout-dev and checkout-prod namespaces only (no wildcards)
AppProject clusterResourceWhitelist is empty or limited to Namespace only
AppProject has namespaceResourceBlacklist blocking ResourceQuota and/or LimitRange
AppProject has orphanedResources.warn: true
AppProject includes a role for checkout-developers group with scoped permissions (not wildcard actions)
Output includes kind: Application with source.path pointing to a Kustomize overlay (overlays/dev or similar)
Application has syncPolicy with automated, prune, selfHeal, and retry
Application uses project: checkout (references the generated AppProject)
Application destination is scoped to the correct namespace (checkout-dev)

evals.name: Helm chart team onboarding
evals.prompt: Onboard the 'analytics' team. They deploy a Helm chart from an OCI registry at oci://registry.acme.com/charts/analytics-dashboard. The chart version is 3.2.0 and they have a values file in a separate Git repo at https://github.com/acme/analytics-config.git under values/production.yaml. They need namespace 'analytics-prod'. SSO group is 'analytics-team'. Generate the onboarding resources.
expectations: Output includes kind: AppProject with sourceRepos containing both the OCI registry URL and the Git config repo
AppProject destinations scoped to analytics-prod namespace
Output includes kind: Application with multi-source configuration (sources array, not single source)
One source references the OCI Helm chart with chart name and targetRevision: 3.2.0
Another source references the Git config repo with values file path
The Helm source uses $values reference to pull values from the Git source
Application has syncPolicy with automated sync and retry
Application uses project: analytics (references the AppProject)
Does NOT generate a Kustomize-based Application — respects the team's Helm-based workflow

evals.name: Self-service ApplicationSet onboarding
evals.prompt: We've onboarded 3 teams manually and it's becoming a bottleneck. Set up a self-service onboarding system where teams can add themselves by submitting a PR to our GitOps repo at https://github.com/acme/platform-config.git. Each team needs an AppProject and an Application. Show me the teams.yaml config format, the ApplicationSet that reads it, and explain the workflow.
expectations: Output includes a teams.yaml schema with fields for team name, repos, namespaces, source type, SSO group
Output includes kind: ApplicationSet with a git-file generator pointing to teams.yaml
ApplicationSet uses goTemplate: true for flexible templating
ApplicationSet template generates an AppProject per team entry (or an Application that creates one)
ApplicationSet template generates an Application per team entry
The generated AppProject has scoped sourceRepos and destinations from the teams.yaml data
Explains the PR-based workflow: team submits PR → platform review → merge → resources created
Mentions what platform team controls (base templates, quota tiers) vs what teams customize
Does NOT generate a monolithic Application that manages all teams — uses per-team isolation

evals.name: Discovery: detect existing setup and fill gaps
evals.prompt: I already have an Argo CD cluster with two AppProjects: 'team-frontend' and 'team-backend'. The frontend team has their Application running fine. Now I need to onboard a third team called 'data-eng'. They use Kustomize, their repo is https://github.com/acme/data-pipelines.git, they need namespace 'data-eng', and their SSO group is 'data-engineers'. The namespace already exists with a ResourceQuota. Don't create things that already exist.
expectations: Does NOT generate Namespace or ResourceQuota — recognizes they already exist
Does NOT modify existing AppProjects (team-frontend, team-backend)
Generates a NEW AppProject for data-eng with appropriate restrictions
Generates a NEW Application pointing to the data-pipelines repo
Asks or confirms what already exists before generating — demonstrates discovery-first behavior
Does NOT generate a blanket 'create everything from scratch' response
Output is minimal — only the delta (AppProject + Application + RBAC for the new team)

evals.name: Refuse overprivileged onboarding
evals.prompt: Onboard the 'platform-ops' team with full cluster access. They need sourceRepos: '*' because they deploy from many repos that change frequently. Give them clusterResourceWhitelist with group: '*' kind: '*' so they can manage CRDs and ClusterRoles. And set their project role to applications: * with exec permission so they can debug pods.
expectations: Does NOT generate wildcard sourceRepos: '*' — explains the security risk
Does NOT generate wildcard clusterResourceWhitelist '*/*' — explains privilege escalation
Does NOT grant exec permission — explains pod access risk
Provides the least-privilege alternative for each request
For 'many repos that change': suggests listing repos explicitly or using a regex pattern if supported
For 'manage CRDs': suggests scoping to specific CRD groups the team actually needs
For 'debug pods': suggests kubectl exec with scoped RBAC instead of Argo CD exec
Tone is helpful — explains WHY each request is dangerous and provides the right alternative

evals.name: Multi-env onboarding with gitops-promoter
evals.prompt: Onboard the 'payments' team with a proper dev → staging → production promotion flow. They use Kustomize, their repo is https://github.com/acme/payments.git. They need 3 environments with gitops-promoter gating promotions between them. Dev should auto-sync, staging should auto-promote after health checks pass, and production should require manual approval. Show me all the Argo CD resources and the promoter configuration.
expectations: Generates an AppProject with destinations for all 3 namespaces (payments-dev, payments-staging, payments-prod)
Generates 3 Applications (or an ApplicationSet) — one per environment
Each Application uses a different targetRevision (environment-specific branches, not all on main)
Does NOT put all 3 Applications on targetRevision: main with different paths only — that defeats promotion
Output includes a PromotionStrategy with 3 environments using branch-based promotion
PromotionStrategy has activeCommitStatuses for health gating (at least pipeline-status and argocd-health)
Production environment has autoMerge: false for manual approval
Dev and staging have autoMerge: true (with commit status gates)
Explains the branch-per-environment model: pipeline commits to dev branch, promoter merges to staging, then to prod
